title: Boat Rental in Cape Town: Tips & Prices for 2026
html:
Set off from the V&A Waterfront in a centre-console for a morning on Table Bay and you will find boat hire Cape Town starting from around 2,500 ZAR for a half-day self-drive. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners across the Western Cape, making it simple to compare options and secure a booking for your desired date.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cape Town?

Prices range from 1,200 ZAR per person on a shared tour to 18,000 ZAR for a full-day crewed catamaran. The exact cost depends on boat size, duration, and whether you take the helm or hire a skipper. For example, your own party of four can charter a 5-metre RIB for four hours from about 3,500 ZAR, while a luxury motor yacht for twelve guests typically starts at 12,000 ZAR for a day. Fuel is usually extra on self-drive rentals, so factor in approximately 800, 1,500 ZAR depending on the route.

  • Shared boat tour: 1,200, 2,000 ZAR per person, 2, 3 hours
  • Small motorboat (half-day self-drive): 2,500, 4,500 ZAR total
  • Catamaran sunset cruise for up to 12: 8,000, 14,000 ZAR (full-day)
  • Crewed yacht with skipper and host: from 15,000 ZAR per day

You can compare verified boat listings and directly message owners through boat hire Cape Town on BoatHire24 to confirm the all-in rate for your preferred vessel.

Which boat to rent in Cape Town?

  1. Centre-console RIB or rigid inflatable, best for speed, fishing, and accessing the islands off Hout Bay; typically 4, 6 guests; from 3,200 ZAR half-day
  2. Bowrider or deck boat, ideal for family days on calm water in Clifton or Camps Bay; up to 8 guests; from 4,000 ZAR half-day
  3. Catamaran, spacious for sunset cruises and champagne picnics; 10, 12 guests; from 8,000 ZAR for a 4-hour trip
  4. Luxury motor yacht, fully crewed for a special occasion or business entertainment; 12 guests and overnight possible; from 15,000 ZAR per day

A popular self-drive choice in 2026 has been the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP listing, offering a 6-metre fibre-glass hull with a four-stroke outboard that makes exploring the shoreline effortless.

Where to set off in Cape Town

Most charters depart from the V&A Waterfront, where the marina sits just beneath Table Mountain. Other good launch points include the Royal Cape Yacht Club in Granger Bay, the marina at Hout Bay Harbour, and the Simon’s Town jetty in False Bay. For a change of scenery, try the smaller jetty at Gordon’s Bay on the shores of the Helderberg mountains.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cape Town?

South Africa requires a valid skippers licence to operate a motorboat independently, but many owners include a licensed skipper in the price. If you hold an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a local SAMSA licence, you can self-drive. Otherwise, the operator confirms exact requirements and will often supply a skipper at an additional 500, 1,000 ZAR per hour.

Tip: book a skipper for your first trip out of the V&A. They will point out the seal colony on Duiker Island and the best angle for a photo of Table Mountain from the sea.

At a glance

  • Price band: 1,200 ZAR (shared) to 18,000 ZAR (crewed day charter)
  • Peak season: November to April (summer), watch for the south-easterly ‘Cape Doctor’ wind
  • Trip length: 2 to 8 hours typical
  • Max capacity: 12 guests on most charter boats
  • Currency: South African Rand (ZAR)

Questions about boat rental in Cape Town

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Cape Town?

Yes, you can book a crewed charter where a licensed skipper operates the vessel while you relax and enjoy the views.

What is the best time of day to cruise Table Bay?

Morning trips between 09:00 and 12:00 offer the calmest water, while late afternoon sunset sails provide dramatic light on Lion’s Head.

How far is Boulders Beach from the V&A Waterfront by boat?

It is about 35 nautical miles south around the Cape Peninsula, a popular full-day trip that often stops in Simon’s Town for lunch.

Do I need to tip the crew?

Tipping is not compulsory but 10, 15% of the charter fee is appreciated if the service is good, especially on crewed yachts.

Before you set sail, check the wind forecast and ask about safety equipment. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cape Town or list your own. List your boat on the platform and join the community of verified hosts in South Africa and beyond.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cape Town and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
